heroHeadline: US-Israel Strike Pasteur Institute on Iran War Day 35; Gaza Phase 2 Stalled; IDF Battalion Suspended Over CNN Detention
heroSubtitle: Day 35 of the US-Israel war on Iran saw strikes on the century-old Pasteur Institute, a military bridge, and steel plants; Iran's death toll reached 2,076. Gaza Phase 2 ceasefire negotiations remain completely frozen — 689 Palestinians killed since the October 2025 ceasefire, food prices up 305%, and 1.6 million Gazans face acute food insecurity. IDF Chief Zamir suspended a reserve battalion after its soldiers assaulted a CNN crew in the West Bank and warned the military could 'collapse on itself' across five simultaneous fronts.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, IDF, Gaza Ministry of Health, B'Tselem, ICC, ICJ, Reuters, Al Jazeera, OHCHR, UNRWA, Yesh Din, Human Rights Watch, and institutional analyses. Casualty figures are subject to ongoing verification. The conflict has persisted since Israeli independence on May 14, 1948.
